title: 12 Days / 11 Nights
price: • After breakfast, check out from the hotel and proceed to Kaziranga National Park. The park, located in  Assam's floodplains of the Brahmaputra River, is home to tigers, elephants, and the world's largest population of Indian one-horned rhinoceroses.

days.title: Arrival in Guwahati & Sightseeing
activities: Welcome to Guwahati! Your adventurous journey awaits.

On arrival at Guwahati Airport/Railway Station and warm greetings, you will proceed to your hotel  in Guwahati. Guwahati is a sprawling city beside the Brahmaputra River in Assam, known for holy sites like the hilltop Kamakhya Temple, which features shrines dedicated to Hindu deities like Shiva and  Vishnu.
Later, you visit Kalakshetra.
In the evening, you can enjoy a Brahmaputra River Cruise (additional  ticket charges) and return to the hotel for an overnight stay.
Places Covered: Kamakhya Temple, Brahmaputra River Cruise(at your own expense)

days.title: Guwahati to Dirang
activities: After breakfast, start your journey to Dirang, a small hill station in West Kameng district. The spectacular landscape and serene nature will soothe your body and soul.
You will visit Kalachakra Gompa  (Dirang Monastery) and explore the local flora, including kiwi orchards. Continue to Dirang Dzong, a  150-year-old fort offering excellent views of the surroundings.
Upon arrival in Dirang, check-in at your hotel.
In the evening, visit the hot water springs, regional sheep farms, Sangti, and the Yak Research  Center.
You will have an overnight stay in Dirang.
Places covered: Kalachakra Gompa  (Dirang Monastery), Dirang Dzong(fort), hot water springs, regional sheep farms, Sangti, the Yak Research Center

days.title: Dirang Sightseeing
activities: After breakfast, drive to Sangti Valley (about 15 km from Dirang), known for its breathtaking beauty and wide variety of fruits like apples, kiwis, corn, and green leafy vegetables. Sangti Valley is also popular among birdwatchers due to the migratory birds that frequent the area.
Afterward, head to  Mandala Hilltop, famous for birdwatching and home to 108 stupas.
You will also visit Lubrang Valley and the Dirang Monastery before returning to Dirang for the night.
Places covered: Sangti valley exploration, Mandala hill top, Lubrang valley, Dirang monastery

days.title: Dirang to Tawang
activities: After breakfast, proceed on a scenic drive to Tawang, passing through the beautiful Sela Pass — the  only route connecting Tawang to the rest of India. Enjoy views of the snow-covered peaks of the eastern Himalayas.
Visit the Sela Lake en route.
Upon arrival, visit the Tawang Monastery and check in to your hotel for an overnight stay.
Places covered: Sela Pass, Tawang Monastery

days.title: Tawang Exploration
activities: Post breakfast, you will visit Shonga-tser Lake (Madhuri Lake), surrounded by snow-capped mountains.
Then head to Bum La Pass (15,200 ft above sea level), which lies at the Indo-China border.
On your way  back, stop at Heart Lake, PTSO Lake, and Naghula Lake. Afterward, return to your hotel in Tawang.
In the evening, visit the Tawang War Memorial followed by an overnight stay in Tawang.
Places covered: Shonga-tser Lake (Madhuri Lake), Bum La Pass, Heart Lake, PTSO Lake, and Naghula Lake, and Tawang War Memorial

days.title: Tawang to Bomdila via Jung Falls
activities: After breakfast, drive to Bomdila, visiting Nag Temple, Gontse Garden, and Rabgye Ling Monastery en route. Also, stop at Nurang Waterfalls (Jung Falls). Then, you will reach Bomdila.
Upon arrival in Bomdila, check in at your hotel.
Visit the museum to learn about the local culture and stay overnight in Bomdila.
Places covered: Nag Temple, Gontse Garden, and Rabgye Ling Monastery, Nurang Waterfalls (Jung Falls), Craft Center and Museum

days.title: Bomdila to Kaziranga
activities: Upon arrival, check in at your hotel.
In the evening, enjoy a cultural program at the park.
Return for overnight stay in Kaziranga.
Places covered: Cultural program at Kaziranga National Park

days.title: Kaziranga National Park - Shillong
activities: Early morning, head out for an elephant ride (additional cost) in one of the park’s zones. Later, explore another zone of the park on a jeep safari (additional cost).
After breakfast, check out and proceed to  Shillong. En route, stop at Umiam Lake (Barapani), and take a ride on a speedboat (additional cost) to  enjoy the scenic beauty.
Upon arrival in Shillong, check in at your hotel and stay overnight.
Places covered: Umiam lake, Jeep Safari and Elephant safari at Kaziranga National Park

days.title: Shillong to Cherrapunjee
activities: After breakfast, you will travel to Cherrapunjee.
En route, visit Shillong Peak (extra cost for taxi) for panoramic  views of the city.
You will visit Elephant Falls, one of the most popular waterfalls in the region and Nohkalikai  Falls, the highest waterfall in India.
Upon arrival in Cherrapunjee, if time allows, visit Mawkdok Dympep Valley Viewpoint and Mawsmai Cave.
Overnight stay in Cherrapunjee.
Places covered: Elephant Falls, Nohkalikai  Falls, Mawkdok Dympep Valley Viewpoint, Mawsmai Cave.

days.title: Cherrapunjee Local Sightseeing
activities: Early morning, embark on a trek to the Double Decker Root Bridge in Nongriat, located in the dense  tropical forest. The trek takes around 5-6 hours round trip. You can also extend your hike to Rainbow Falls  (about a 60 minutes hike from Nongriat).
Alternatively, explore other attractions in Cherrapunjee, including  Seven Sisters Waterfall, Eco Park, Wei Sawdong Falls, Arwah Cave, Wah-Kaba Falls, and the Garden of  Caves.
Return to your hotel in the evening followed by an overnight stay in Cherrapunjee.
Places covered:  Trek to Double Decker Root Bridge in Nongriat, Seven Sisters Waterfall, Eco Park, Wei Sawdong Falls, Arwah Cave, Wah-Kaba Falls, Garden of  Caves.

days.title: Cherrapunjee – Mawlynnong – Dawki (Shnongpdeng) - Shillong
activities: After breakfast, travel to Mawlynnong, the cleanest village in Asia (awarded in 2003).
Explore the village  on foot, visiting Balancing Rock, the Sky Viewpoint (Nohwet Viewpoint), and the longest living root  bridge.

Enjoy a local lunch in the village before proceeding to Dawki, a border town known for its crystal clear Umngot river. Enjoy a boating experience (additional cost). Return to Shillong and check in at your  hotel for an overnight stay.
Places covered: Mawlynnong, Balancing Rock, the Sky Viewpoint (Nohwet Viewpoint), Umngot River, Boating in Umngot river(at your own expense).

days.title: Shillong – Guwahati
activities: After breakfast, check out from your hotel and drive back to Guwahati.
Upon arrival, you can visit local markets for shopping.
Later, transfer to the Guwahati Airport / Railway Station for your onward journey.
Voila! Your beautiful journey ends here, take tons of memories along and cherish them for a lifetime.
Important things to Know:
Weather: Prepare for unpredictable weather, especially at high altitudes.
Altitude: Tawang’s high altitude can cause sickness; stay hydrated.
Road Conditions: Roads can be rough and travel time longer in some areas.
Connectivity: Limited mobile network in remote areas.
Permits: Special permits required for some locations (Tawang, Bumla Pass).
Packing: Bring weather-appropriate clothes, medications, and essentials.
Culture: Respect local customs, especially at religious sites.

title: 6 Days / 5 Nights
price: • Upon arrival, you check into your eco-stay and head out for an afternoon jeep safari or elephant ride, offering close-up views of rhinos, wild boars, and migratory birds.

days.title: Arrival in Guwahati | Drive to Manas National Park
activities: Your journey begins in Guwahati, where the city’s bustle fades as you head west toward the lush forests of Manas National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site nestled along the Bhutan border. The drive takes you through quiet villages, tea gardens, and river valleys.
By afternoon, you arrive at your forest lodge, surrounded by sal trees and the calls of distant hornbills. The air is thick with anticipation — tomorrow, the jungle awaits.
You spend the evening soaking in the serenity, perhaps with a short nature walk or fireside storytelling under the stars.
Overnight stay in Manas National Park.
days.highlight: Scenic drive into the Himalayan foothills and forest retreat

days.title: Safari in Manas National Park
activities: At first light, you set out on a jeep safari through the pristine wilderness of Manas.

The park is home to rare species like the golden langur, pygmy hog, and wild water buffalo, along with elephants, rhinos, and tigers. The forest feels  untouched — tall grasslands, riverine patches, and dense jungle create a mosaic of habitats. You may spot peacocks dancing in the morning mist or herds of deer grazing by the river.
After lunch, you can opt for a second safari or simply relax at your lodge, listening to the forest breathe.
Overnight stay in Manas National Park.
days.highlight: Jeep safari through the national park

days.title: From Foothills to Floodplains – Manas to Kaziranga
activities: After breakfast, you bid farewell to Manas and begin your journey eastward toward Kaziranga National Park, Assam’s crown jewel of conservation.
The drive takes you across the Brahmaputra plains, past tea estates and river crossings.
By late afternoon, you arrive at your lodge near Kaziranga, where the landscape opens into vast grasslands and wetlands.
The evening is yours to unwind — perhaps with a visit to the nearby orchid park or a quiet sunset walk.
Overnight stay in Kaziranga National Park.

days.title: Safari in Kaziranga National Park
activities: Today begins with a Jeep Safari through Kaziranga’s central or western range. The park is famed for its one-horned rhinoceroses, often seen grazing in the tall elephant grass. You may also spot wild elephants, swamp deer, and a dazzling array of birds.
The wetlands shimmer in the morning light, and the calls of bar-headed geese and pelicans echo across the water.
In the afternoon, you can opt for another safari or visit a nearby tribal village to learn about local life and crafts.
Overnight stay in Kaziranga National Park.
days.highlight: Wildlife safari in Kaziranga’s rhino-rich grassland

days.title: Kaziranga to Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary
activities: After breakfast, you leave Kaziranga and drive toward Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary, a lesser-known gem located just outside Guwahati.
Though smaller in size, Pobitora boasts one of the highest densities of one-horned rhinos in the world.
Overnight stay in Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary.
days.highlight: Rhino sightings & Jeep Safari in Pobitora

days.title: Pobitora to Guwahati | Departure
activities: Your final morning begins with a relaxed breakfast and perhaps a short nature walk through Pobitora’s buffer zone. The mist lifts slowly off the grasslands, revealing water birds and grazing rhinos one last time.
Then, it’s time to drive back to Guwahati, where your journey ends — but the memories of jungle trails, river crossings, and wild encounters stay with you.
